Name: spectral_routing_optimizer
Title: Spectral Routing Optimizer: A Curvature-Aware Precision Routing Framework for Efficient Inference
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can dynamically allocate numerical precision based on real-time activation geometry, leading to significant latency and memory gains without sacrificing accuracy. By leveraging spectral quantization theory and low-overhead implementation, we can develop a training-free framework that adapts to dynamic input distributions and enforces strict latency budgets.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works in spectral quantization routing (SQR) and spectral latent steering (SLS), which have demonstrated the potential of geometric diagnostics and precision switching for efficient inference. However, these methods rely on static profiling or costly calibration, failing to adapt to dynamic input distributions. Our approach addresses this limitation by introducing a curvature-aware precision routing framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on real-time activation geometry.
Abstract: We propose a spectral routing optimizer, a curvature-aware precision routing framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on real-time activation geometry. Our approach leverages spectral quantization theory and low-overhead implementation to develop a training-free framework that adapts to dynamic input distributions and enforces strict latency budgets. We evaluate our framework on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks and demonstrate a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, our approach provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
Experiments: Evaluate the spectral routing optimizer on WILDS benchmarks using single-GPU setups
Compare the performance of our framework with static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines
Assess the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on latency and memory usage
Investigate the effect of spectral quantization theory on the stability and generalization of the model
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the spectral routing optimizer may lead to increased computational overhead
The framework may not adapt well to highly dynamic or adversarial input distributions
The use of spectral quantization theory may introduce additional hyperparameters that require careful tuning
